About Me

From econometrics to the factory floor and back to the numbers

I'm Sarah Grattapaglia, an Operations Controller with a background in Statistics and Econometrics, currently working at Marcolin Group. I hold a Double Degree Master's in Industrial Organizations & Innovation from the University of Pavia and the University of Tübingen.

I specialize in turning data into decisions by combining analytical thinking, cost controlling, financial reporting, and process automation. My work spans the entire analytics lifecycle, from collecting and validating data to analysing performance, identifying opportunities, and delivering insights that support business decisions.

Beyond my professional role, I build AI and automation projects to improve efficiency, reduce manual work, and explore practical applications of technology in finance and controlling.

🎓
Master's, Industrial Organizations & Innovation (Econometrics)
Double Degree Program, University of Pavia and University of Tübingen, 2022 to 2024 · +25 extra-curricular ECTS in econometrics · Thesis: sectorial M&A differences in Germany, 1997 to 2023 · Final grade 106/110

What drives me
Solving business problems through data analysis, automation, and technology, and being rigorous and curious enough to keep learning the tools that make that possible.
Experience

Professional Journey

Operational controlling, revenue management, and strategic consulting, three angles on the same core skill of turning raw data into a decision someone can act on.

Junior Operations Controller
Feb 2026 to Present
Marcolin Group

Operational controlling for a manufacturing business, covering inventory, cost, and budget discipline at the point where finance meets the warehouse floor.

  • Contributed to monthly closing by monitoring inventory valuation, provisions, and warehouse performance, supporting accurate and timely financial reporting.
  • Carried out product cost calculations and variance analysis on freight and operational costs, supporting cost control and management decision-making.
  • Supported budget reviews and rolling forecast cycles, tracking Top Line and Opex performance against plan.
SAP Advanced Excel Variance Analysis Product Costing
Junior Analyst, On Board Revenue
Mar 2025 to Jan 2026
Costa Crociere

Revenue management across on-board categories on cruise ships, with pricing decisions made visible and trackable through dashboards, not gut feel.

  • Managed dynamic pricing across multiple on-board revenue categories, monitoring KPI performance against plan and refining pricing strategies accordingly.
  • Built Power BI dashboards and monthly sales reports to track revenue performance and support management reporting.
  • Analysed actual vs. planned revenue variances and produced regular performance summaries for commercial and finance teams.
Power BI Dynamic Pricing Variance Analysis Revenue Reporting
Business Analyst Intern
Nov 2024 to Mar 2025
RINA

First exposure to strategic consulting, building the analytical scaffolding senior management uses to make cross-business-unit decisions.

  • Contributed to digital transformation and strategic projects across multiple Business Units, building business cases and scenario analyses to support senior management in strategic decision-making.
Business Cases Scenario Analysis Digital Transformation
Assistant Professor, Mathematics & Statistics
2022 to 2023
University

Where the analytical foundation was put to its first real test: explaining quantitative concepts clearly enough for someone else to build on.

  • Delivered academic support in Mathematics and Statistics, designing personalised study plans to improve students' quantitative performance.
Statistics Teaching Mentoring

Beyond Work

Off the clock

The habits and commitments that shape who I am, quite separate from any job title.

🩸
Italian Red Cross Volunteer

Active volunteer with the Italian Red Cross since 2021, supporting community and emergency-response activities.

It keeps me grounded, and it's a reminder that composure under pressure and showing up reliably matter, no matter the setting.

Teamwork Resilience Composure Under Pressure
🌍
Erasmus+ Exchange & Training

Participated in Erasmus+ exchange and training projects across multiple EU countries since 2021.

Spending time alongside people from completely different backgrounds is the fastest way I know to get comfortable with ambiguity.

Adaptability Cross-Cultural Communication Curiosity
🏃
Gym, Running & Weekend Trekking

Regular gym sessions and running during the week, and on weekends, I organize trekking outings.

Sticking to a training schedule and planning a trek from start to finish are both exercises in discipline and follow-through.

Discipline Planning & Organization Physical & Mental Resilience
🏍️
Motorbiking in the Dolomites

I ride motorbikes, with the Dolomites as a favorite route.

Riding on mountain roads demands full attention and calculated decision-making in the moment, there's no room for autopilot.

Decision-Making Focus Risk Assessment
